headline: Emergency Ordinance detainees exceed 600 under remand
context: Over 600 individuals remained detained under various Emergency Ordinance provisions as of 2025, despite states of emergency having ended.

cards.text: Over 600 people detained under Emergency Ordinances after emergencies ended
cards.sub: Preventive detention continues under laws that should have lapsed

cards.h: Emergency (Essential Powers) Ordinance 1969 provisions remain in force via savings clause
cards.s: Federal Court has not definitively ruled on validity of post-emergency detentions
cards.d: Emergency (Essential Powers) Ordinance 1969 provisions remain in force via savings clause Federal Court has not definitively ruled on validity of post-emergency detentions

cards.h: Article 150 emergency powers intended as temporary measures during crises
cards.s: Using emergency laws decades after emergency contradicts constitutional design
cards.d: Article 150 emergency powers intended as temporary measures during crises Using emergency laws decades after emergency contradicts constitutional design

cards.h: 76% of detainees under EO provisions have been held for over 3 years
cards.s: Families describe indefinite detention as destroying livelihoods and mental health
cards.d: 76% of detainees under EO provisions have been held for over 3 years Families describe indefinite detention as destroying livelihoods and mental health

cards.text: 600 people detained under emergency laws with no emergency is normalised injustice
Malaysian Bar passed resolution calling for review of all EO detentions in 2024
